title: dalbergia cumingii var. zollingeriana
intro: Dalbergia cumingii var. zollingeriana, also known as the Philippine rosewood or purpleheart, is a variant of the Dalbergia cumingii tree species native to the Philippines. It is a medicinal plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and other ailments, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as acting as an antimicrobial agent. The plant is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
